_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize statewide-exec batch: Rayfield (OR AG), Davis (PA LG), Sunday (PA AG), Gonzalez-Colon (PR Gov), Neronha (RI AG). State tier (49 cells). reset_unspecified nulls every applicable cell with no cited evidence. Cross-party deviations captured: Rayfield/Neronha tough-on-crime (drug recriminalization / anti-bail+ICE), Sunday federal-deference FALSE + GOA/FOP TRUEs, Gonzalez-Colon socially-conservative TRUEs with two documented LGBTQ deviations (Equality Act, Every Child Deserves a Family Act).

profile.background: 18th Attorney General of Oregon (D), sworn in Dec 31, 2024; former Oregon House Speaker (2022-2024), House member for Corvallis 2015-2024. One of the most active anti-Trump-administration AGs (48+ multistate suits).

dan-rayfield.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Uniformly progressive AG: drove HB 2002 (2023) expanding abortion + gender-affirming care; defends mifepristone; sued to block Planned Parenthood defunding; PP-Oregon + NARAL endorsements. Leads multistate suit defending youth gender-affirming care vs HHS. Defended Oregon abortion-coverage mandate against Oregon Right to Life religious-exemption claim (appealing). Opposed SAVE Act + sued federal citizenship-verification/mail-voting EO. Defends Oregon sanctuary law; sued Trump birthright-citizenship EO (won at 9th Cir). Defended Measure 114 mag-ban/permit; Giffords-endorsed. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (only conservative-aligned data point): as Speaker helped pass HB 4002 (2024) recriminalizing drug possession, calling Measure 110 decriminalization a failure -> public_justice credit. refuse_federal_overreach nulled (his federal resistance serves progressive ends, not conservative federalism).

0.note: Drove HB 2002 expanding abortion access; defends abortion as 'safe, effective' -- does not affirm personhood at conception.

1.note: Sued to block PP defunding; joined mifepristone brief -- opposes abortion restrictions, not an abolitionist.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood PAC of Oregon and NARAL Pro-Choice Oregon (2024).

0.note: Pro-LGBTQ posture; leads pro-trans litigation -- no affirmation of one-man-one-woman marriage.

2.note: Leads 19-state suit defending youth gender-affirming care vs HHS; embraces transgender ideology.

4.note: HB 2002 codified gender protections; sued to protect federal gender-care funding -- promotion of LGBTQ identity in policy.

1.note: HB 2002 removed parental-consent requirements for minors seeking abortion/gender care under certain conditions.
Defended Oregon's abortion-coverage insurance mandate against Oregon Right to Life's religious-employer exemption claim; announced appeal -- opposes conscience exemption.

4.note: ESG/climate-aligned litigation posture (Coinbase securities suit, tariff litigation); no anti-ESG record.

1.note: Opposed the SAVE Act and sued the federal citizenship-verification EO -- opposes voter-ID + citizenship verification.

3.note: Sued to permanently block restrictions on mail voting -- supports mass mail-in voting.

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: as Speaker helped pass HB 4002 (2024) recriminalizing drug possession, calling Measure 110 decriminalization a failure -- reversed a soft-on-crime regime.
Defends Oregon sanctuary law; sued birthright-citizenship EO -- opposes mass deportation enforcement.

2.note: Moved to dismiss county suit seeking ICE cooperation; defends Oregon's ~40-year sanctuary law.

4.note: Sued to block Trump birthright-citizenship EO and won at 9th Circuit -- supports birthright citizenship.

1.note: Defended Measure 114 (permit-to-purchase + 10-round magazine ban); Giffords-endorsed; advanced ghost-gun ban -- supports magazine limits and registries.

profile.background: 35th Lieutenant Governor of Pennsylvania (D), in office since Jan 2023 with Gov. Josh Shapiro; former PA House member (District 35, Allegheny County, 2018-2022). Chairs the PA Commission on Crime and Delinquency. Running for re-election (May 2026 primary).

austin-davis.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Uniformly progressive record (no conservative deviations found). House votes: NO on SB 106 (constitutional abortion-exclusion), NO on Down-syndrome/fetal-remains abortion restrictions; sponsored reproductive-liberty + full-abortion-coverage bills (PA Choice Tracker 'Pro-Choice Champion'). LGBTQ Equality Caucus, co-sponsored the Fairness Act; HRC-endorsed; backed conversion-therapy ban. Gun-control advocate (ghost guns, universal background checks, red-flag); Everytown/Moms Demand ally; chairs PCCD/Office of Gun Violence Prevention with public-health 'epidemic' framing. Pro-mail-voting/Act 77. Opposed proposed ICE detention facilities in Berks/Schuylkill. Christian-liberty, economic-stewardship (CBDC/ESG/antitrust), family-sovereignty cells nulled -- no evidence found.

0.note: Voted NO on SB 106 (excluding abortion right from PA Constitution); sponsored a 'reproductive liberty' constitutional amendment -- does not affirm personhood.

1.note: PA Choice Tracker 'Pro-Choice Champion'; sponsored full-abortion-coverage bills -- opposes restrictions, not an abolitionist.

0.note: Member of the PA House LGBTQ Equality Caucus; co-sponsored the Fairness Act -- does not affirm one-man-one-woman marriage.

2.note: Backed banning conversion therapy for minors and eliminating the gay/trans-panic defense -- embraces transgender ideology.

4.note: Co-sponsored the Fairness Act (SOGI anti-discrimination); HRC-endorsed -- promotion of LGBTQ identity in policy.

3.note: Supports no-excuse mail-in voting (Act 77); pledged to protect mail voting -- supports mass mail-in voting.
Consistently frames gun violence as a public-health 'epidemic' -- treats violent crime through the public-health lens the question warns against.

1.note: Vowed to 'halt' proposed ICE detention facilities; 'immigrant communities shouldn't have to live in fear' -- opposes deportation enforcement.
Prominent gun-control advocate: ghost-gun ban, universal background checks, red-flag laws; Everytown/Moms Demand ally.

profile.background: Attorney General of Pennsylvania (R), in office since Jan 2025; former York County District Attorney (2018-2025); Navy veteran. Deliberately non-activist 'boring AG' who refuses to sue the Trump administration AND declines to join Republican-AG multistate suits.

dave-sunday.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Narrow public record by design. STRONG documented conservative credentials where evidence exists: Fraternal Order of Police + PA State Troopers Association endorsements, career prosecutor (10+ murder convictions, death penalty for qualifying crimes); Gun Owners of America endorsement (against universal registration checks, against red-flag confiscation, pro-firearms-preemption). Defended PA's Medicaid-abortion-funding ban in court on rule-of-law grounds but pointedly refused to make abortion an AG cause, declined to state a personal view, pledged never to prosecute women (no pro-life endorsement found -> sanctity nulled). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: deferential to federal authority -- said the federal government 'has a right' to cut funding ('that's my position') and stayed out of both GOP and Democratic multistate litigation, including the mail-voting suit Gov. Shapiro had to join in his place -> refuse_federal_overreach FALSE on state-sovereignty. Silent on marriage/sexuality/family/Christian-liberty/immigration -> nulled. NOTE: York DA record is heavily treatment/diversion-oriented (Opioid Collaborative, CARD, mental-health diversion, ~40% prison-population reduction), tempering the law-and-order read.

0.note: Endorsed by PA State Lodge Fraternal Order of Police, York FOP No. 73, and the PA State Troopers Association; career prosecutor -- publicly backs law enforcement.

3.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: deferential to federal authority -- 'The federal government has a right to cut fraud, waste, and abuse... that's their right, and that's my position'; declines GOP-AG sovereignty suits. Does not assert state sovereignty against federal action.

1.note: Gun Owners of America endorsement: AGAINST universal registration checks and red-flag confiscation orders; strong supporter of PA firearms-preemption law (helped stop a York city gun ordinance).

profile.background: Governor of Puerto Rico (took office Jan 2, 2025). New Progressive Party (PNP, pro-statehood); on the U.S. mainland party axis a Republican -- former Resident Commissioner (non-voting U.S. House) 2017-2025, PR GOP chair 2015-2021, Trump endorser. Note: as Resident Commissioner she had no final-passage floor vote, so her congressional record is sponsorship-based.

jenniffer-gonzalez-colon.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Genuinely MIXED record -- socially conservative with two documented LGBTQ deviations. CONSERVATIVE (HARD): signed Law 183-2025 + Law 18-2026 recognizing the unborn as a person from conception; signed Act 63-2025 banning gender-affirming care under 21 (over her own Health Secretary's objection -- among the strictest in any U.S. jurisdiction); signed Law 122-2025 parental consent for minors' abortion; signed Act 14-2025 religious-freedom law + created a Faith-Based Affairs office (EO 2025-020); signed a new Police Law granting law-enforcement autonomy; cooperated with federal immigration enforcement (DTOP data-sharing with HSI, ICE airport support). DEVIATIONS (HARD, flagged): cosponsored the Equality Act (2019, 1 of only 3 House Republicans) and the Every Child Deserves a Family Act (anti-discrimination incl. SOGI in adoption -> opposes faith-based-agency protections) -> biblical_marriage q4 + family q4 FALSE. Abortion remains legal in PR (personhood laws largely symbolic) so abortion-abolition cell nulled. refuse_federal_overreach nulled: she chose compliance over resistance ('we cannot choose which laws we follow') and seeks statehood (deeper federal integration). Self-defense / election-integrity / no-fault-divorce: no evidence -> nulled.

0.note: Signed Law 183-2025 and Law 18-2026 recognizing the unborn child as a natural person from conception.

4.note: Catholic, pro-life; SBA Pro-Life praised her personhood law; no abortion-industry money or endorsement found.

2.note: Signed Act 63-2025 banning gender-affirming care for anyone under 21, even with parental consent, over her Health Secretary's objection -- rejects transgender ideology for minors.

4.note: DEVIATION: cosponsored the Equality Act (2019), one of only 3 House Republicans, adding SOGI protections to civil-rights law -- promotion of LGBTQ identity in policy.

1.note: Signed Law 122-2025 requiring parental informed consent for minors seeking abortion.

4.note: DEVIATION: cosponsored the Every Child Deserves a Family Act, which bars federally funded child-welfare agencies from declining placements on religious/SOGI grounds -- opposite of protecting faith-based adoption agencies.

0.note: Signed Act 14-2025 establishing religious freedom as government public policy; openly Catholic and public about faith -- affirms the right to publicly profess faith.

3.note: Created the Office of the Governor's Advisor on Faith-Based Affairs (EO 2025-020); held faith-based inauguration meeting -- supports religion in the public square.

0.note: Signed the new Puerto Rico Police Law granting greater fiscal/operational autonomy; certified new police classes -- backs law enforcement.

2.note: Directed PR agencies (DTOP) to share resident data with federal HSI/ICE and supported ICE operations -- opposes sanctuary policy, cooperates with federal preemption.

profile.background: Attorney General of Rhode Island (D), in office since Jan 2019, re-elected 2022; term-limited (leaves Jan 2027). Former U.S. Attorney for Rhode Island. One of the nation's most prolific anti-Trump-administration AGs (~57+ multistate suits).

peter-neronha.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Uniformly progressive on culture/federalism: joined mifepristone amicus + PP-defunding suit; Planned Parenthood Votes! RI PAC endorsement; issued trans-protective/DEI school guidance and sued over youth gender-affirming care; sued the federal citizenship-verification + mail-voting EOs; sued the birthright-citizenship EO; joined anti-commandeering statement + school-ICE guidance; leads RI v. Shell climate-deception suit (ESG); defended RI's large-capacity-magazine ban, 2025 assault-weapons ban, and concealed-carry 'need' requirement. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (only conservative-aligned data point): publicly OPPOSED bail-reform/cashless-bail legislation (bail should remain judicial discretion) AND in the Garcia Dela Cruz case sided with ICE against his own Democratic governor over the release of a child-molestation suspect -> public_justice credit. refuse_federal_overreach nulled (his federal resistance serves progressive ends). Christian-liberty / no-fault-divorce / euthanasia / personhood: no evidence -> nulled.

0.note: Joined 23-AG amicus defending mifepristone access -- pro-abortion-access, does not affirm personhood.

1.note: Sued over the federal Planned Parenthood defund provision -- opposes abortion restrictions, not an abolitionist.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Votes! Rhode Island PAC.

0.note: Issued trans-protective school guidance; pro-LGBTQ posture -- does not affirm one-man-one-woman marriage.

2.note: Co-led multistate suit defending gender-affirming care for minors -- embraces transgender ideology.

4.note: Issued LGBTQ/DEI-protective school guidance -- promotion of LGBTQ identity in schools/policy.

1.note: School guidance affirms trans accommodations without parental-notification requirement -- contrary to parental notification/consent.

2.note: Directed schools to keep DEI programs and SOGI protections -- promotes SOGI in K-12.

4.note: Leads RI v. Shell, the nation's first state climate-deception suit -- ESG/climate-aligned posture.

1.note: Joined 22-AG suit against the federal documentary-proof-of-citizenship voter-registration EO -- opposes voter-ID + citizenship verification.

3.note: Sued to block restrictions on mail voting -- supports mass mail-in voting.

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: opposed bail-reform/cashless-bail legislation (bail should remain judicial discretion); sided with ICE against his own governor over a child-molestation suspect's release -- opposes soft-on-crime policy.
Sued the birthright-citizenship EO; school-ICE-limiting guidance -- opposes aggressive deportation enforcement.

2.note: Joined anti-commandeering statement that state/local LE cannot be used for federal immigration enforcement -- supports sanctuary-type policy.

4.note: Challenged the birthright-citizenship EO -- supports birthright citizenship.

1.note: Defended RI's large-capacity-magazine ban, the 2025 assault-weapons ban, and the concealed-carry 'need' requirement -- supports magazine limits and AWB.
